title: 6.2.4 Phosphorus Cycle
description: Phosphorus cycle — every fact from the Grade 10 Biology textbook.

questions.question: Why is phosphorus needed by organisms?
Besides proteins, of which important molecule is phosphorus an essential component?
What is DNA described as being key for?
For which molecule's synthesis is phosphorus also needed?
options: ATP, the energy currency of a cell
questions.correctAnswer: ATP, the energy currency of a cell

questions.question: ATP stands for what?
What is ATP the immediate source of?
options: Energy (instantly liberates energy) needed for various activities of a cell
questions.correctAnswer: Energy (instantly liberates energy) needed for various activities of a cell

questions.question: How is ATP produced?
options: Energy released during cellular respiration is harvested and stored as ATP
questions.correctAnswer: Energy released during cellular respiration is harvested and stored as ATP

questions.question: In what form is phosphorus usually present in soil and rock?
What are phosphate ions present in soil or water taken up by?
Into what form are phosphate ions converted when taken up by plants and algae?
How is organic phosphate synthesized by producers transferred?
options: Along food chains to various consumers or feeders
questions.correctAnswer: Along food chains to various consumers or feeders

questions.question: What happens when dead bodies of plants and animals are decomposed by bacteria and fungi?
options: Phosphate ions are released from compounds like phospholipids, ATP, and DNA and returned to soil and water
Phosphorus becomes locked in rocks forever
questions.correctAnswer: Phosphate ions are released from compounds like phospholipids, ATP, and DNA and returned to soil and water

questions.question: From which compounds are phosphate ions released during decomposition?
How else is phosphate returned to the environment besides decomposition?
options: Through excretion from animals and aquatic organisms
questions.correctAnswer: Through excretion from animals and aquatic organisms

questions.question: What is one way phosphate enters the soil?
options: In the form of fertilizers obtained by mining phosphate rocks and applying them in agricultural lands
questions.correctAnswer: In the form of fertilizers obtained by mining phosphate rocks and applying them in agricultural lands

questions.question: What is the purpose of applying phosphate fertilizers to agricultural lands?
What can happen when phosphate fertilizer from farmlands is leached by rainfall?
options: It can reach nearby water bodies and cause eutrophication
It becomes permanently trapped in soil
questions.correctAnswer: It can reach nearby water bodies and cause eutrophication

questions.question: What is eutrophication?
options: The natural aging of a lake
Undesirable excessive growth of aquatic plants and massive growth of algae (algal bloom) induced by leaching of fertilizers rich in nitrogen and phosphorus
questions.correctAnswer: Undesirable excessive growth of aquatic plants and massive growth of algae (algal bloom) induced by leaching of fertilizers rich in nitrogen and phosphorus

questions.question: The phosphorus cycle mainly involves the transfer of what?
options: Phosphorus gas from air to water
Phosphate ions from soil, water, and rock to plants and aquatic algae
Organic phosphorus from animals to plants
Phosphorus from the atmosphere to the soil
questions.correctAnswer: Phosphate ions from soil, water, and rock to plants and aquatic algae

questions.question: In the phosphorus cycle, what is first converted in the producers?
How is organic phosphate from producers transferred to consumers?
By what are phosphate ions released from dead bodies of plants and animals?
Which type of fertilizer can cause eutrophication when leached into water bodies?
options: Phosphate fertilizers from farmlands rich in nitrogen and phosphorus
questions.correctAnswer: Phosphate fertilizers from farmlands rich in nitrogen and phosphorus

questions.question: The phosphorus cycle operates over what time frame for some processes?
ATP is also known as what?
options: The energy currency of a cell
questions.correctAnswer: The energy currency of a cell

questions.question: What does ATP stand for in full?
Eutrophication leads to massive growth of which organisms?
What is the main source of phosphate fertilizers?
Which essential plant nutrients in fertilizers cause eutrophication when they leach into water bodies?
